LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS SYNERGENICS, LLC, WILLIAM RUTTER AND STEPHEN HALASZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE AGREEMENT IS NOT MUTUAL GIVEN THE EXCEPTION FOR INJUNCTION ACTIONS, AND DEFENDANT'S BUSINESS JUSTIFICATION FOR THE PROVISION WAS EXPRESSLY REJECTED IN STIRLEN VS. SUPERCUTS (1997) 51 CAL. APP. 4TH 1519. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending Arbitration; Appendix Of Non-California Authorities, Request For Judicial Notice, Declarations Of William J. Rutter, Ph.D. , And Cecily A. Waterman, Esq. In Support
Off Calendar May-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. SCOTT BONAGOFSKY, TEL. NO. 882-1555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTINA M. KOTOWSKI OF THE FISHER & PHILLIPS, LLP, TEL. NO. 490-9019 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS SYNERGENICS, LLC, WILLIAM RUTTER AND STEPHEN HALASZ'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending Arbitration is denied. The agreement is not mutual given the exception for injunction actions, and defendant's business justification for the provision was expressly rejected in Stirlen vs. Supercuts (1997) 51 Cal. App. 4th 1519. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB/AY)
